title: What's Already Here
intention: To warm the heart by noticing the small, real things that are already present.

lines.text: Welcome. Take your seat, and let yourself stop for a while.
Let your eyes close, or rest them low and soft.
Feel the weight of your body where it meets the chair or the floor.
Take one slow breath in. And let it go.
There is nothing to fix here. Just be where you are.
Notice your breath, moving on its own.
Your lungs are working. They have not stopped all day.
Feel the simple fact of air coming in, and going out.
This is already a gift. Easy to miss, and here all along.
Now let your attention move to your hands.
These hands have carried things, opened doors, held what matters.
Let them rest, warm and quiet, in your lap.
Bring to mind one small thing from today that was good.
A warm drink. A familiar voice. A moment of quiet like this one.
Let it be small. Small is enough.
Stay with it. Let the warmth of it settle in your chest.
Take a slow breath, and let that good thing fill the breath.
Notice the floor still holding you. The roof above you.
So much is quietly here, asking nothing of you.
Let a quiet thank you form, just for what is.
Take one more easy breath, and keep this warmth with you.
When you are ready, open your eyes. Carry it gently into your day.
